claim_text: LinkedIn defines employee advocacy as employees promoting the company they work for and frames a program as strategic, sustainable, and voluntary.

claim_text: LinkedIn Help says the My Company tab, Employee Advocacy tab, and curator admin role began being discontinued in November 2024, while Page admins can still reshare employee posts and notify employees about important Page posts.

claim_text: LinkedIn Help says Page admins can notify employees when the organization posts an important Page post, and employees can react, comment, or reshare after clicking the preview.

claim_text: LinkedIn Pages Best Practices recommends notifying employees of the most important Page posts up to one time per week.

notes: Practical cadence recommendation from LinkedIn Business Solutions.

claim_text: LinkedIn individual post analytics include impressions, members reached, profile viewers from the post, followers gained, reactions, comments, reposts, saves, sends, link visits, and viewer demographics, and LinkedIn notes that post analytics are estimates.
The 2025 Edelman-LinkedIn research says 56% of target buyers and 55% of hidden buyers use thought leadership as part of vendor evaluation.

claim_text: Employee advocacy is coordinated distribution and participation, while employee-generated content is employee expertise turned into original public content.

claim_text: Employee advocacy starts with a message the company wants people to help move, while employee-generated content starts with what an employee knows from doing the work.
